message:
fix(core): correct CTAS type affinity inference and compat_persist affinity mappings

CTAS (CREATE TABLE AS SELECT) was hardcoding TEXT affinity ('B') for all
columns, causing integers to be coerced to text during apply_affinity().
Now infers affinity from the first non-NULL value in each result column,
falling back to BLOB affinity ('A') when no data is available.

Also fixes two inverted affinity-char mappings in compat_persist.rs:
- affinity_char_to_type: 'B' mapped to "BLOB" instead of TEXT, 'A' to
  "NUMERIC" instead of BLOB
- type_to_affinity: BLOB/TEXT/NUMERIC chars were swapped and used
  lowercase instead of uppercase

Updates 5 tests that were written against the buggy mappings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
